State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance
(916) 322-5660

322-5901

Enforcement • •
322-6441

Statements of Economic Interest
322-6444

March 29, 1982


Betty M. Pellinen
City Clerk
City Hall
250 East L Street
Benicia, CA 94510

Re: Advice Request
No. 82-050

Dear Ms. Pellinen:

Thank you for your letter requesting advice and assistance
from this office. We do not have enough information to
judge the sufficiency of the filings of the O'Blennis Committee
or the Legal Defense Fund. However, I can provide you with
a general outline of the applicable reporting requirements
of the Political Reform Act which should assist you in your
duties as filing officer. If Mayor O'Blennis would like
more specific advice on the filings required from his committees,
he or his treasurer should contact us directly or through
their legal representative.

The Act requires that controlled committees file semi-
annual campaign statements reporting all contributions and
expenditures during the reporting period. Government Code
Section 84200.— In addition, threshold campaign statements
are required if the committee's activities exceed certain
limits outlined in Section 84200(b). On all of these statements,
contributions and expenditures of $100 or more must be itemized.

Amounts collected and spent for legal fees and costs
are generally reportable if the lawsuit relates to the election
process or the official's status as an officeholder. See
Opinion requested by Douglas Buchanan, 5 FPPC Opinions 14
(No. 78-013, May 1, 1979). It appears that the funds collected


1/
Unless statutory references are to the Government Code
otherwise noted.

Betty M. Pellinen
March 29, 1982
Page Two


and expended by the Mayor's committee for the defense of the
lawsuit brought to remove him from office would be reportable
as contributions and expenditures.

On the Mayor's annual Statement of Economic Interest,
he must report all interests in real property, investments,
and income. Section 87203. Income does not include political
contributions or expenditures for legal services (which are
reportable on the committee's campaign statements). Section
82030(b)(1). Income does include gifts from any source
including gifts from a political committee. Sections 82028
and 82030(a).

I hope this discussion has been helpful to you. If I
can be of further assistance, please feel free to contact me
at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Diane Maura Fishburn
Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh
cc: David Davenport


2/ In this regard, please see Election Code Section
12402 which prohibits the personal use of campaign funds;
Any questions concerning the interpretation of this section
should be directed to the Attorney General's Office or the
Solano County District Attorney.

JOHN F SILVA
CITY MANAGER

BETTY M. PELLINEN
CITY CLERK

PHYLLIS G. GARRIGUES
CITY TREASURER

OFFICE OF

City Clerk

WARREN R. O'BLENNIS
MAYOR

COUNCIL MEMBERS

PEPE ARTEAGA
JOHN F CODY
ROGER E LIPMAN
GARY WINSTON

City of Benicia

CITY HALL
250 EAST L STREET
BENICIA, CALIFORNIA
94510

WHERE CALIFORNIA'S FIRST STATE CAPITOL STILL STANDS

AREA CODE (707) 745-0510

March 12, 1982

Ms. Jean Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca  95814

Dear Jean:

     As per our telephone conversation, I am sending you the
Statement of Organization and Recipient Committee Campaign
Statement filed by David Davenport on behalf of "Warren
O'Blennis Legal Defense Fund"; also, the letter mailed by
Warren R. O'Blennis, Mayor (Paid for by O'Blennis Committee).
The letter made reference to the Recall having nothing to do
with his residence (I am also sending you a copy of the
"Notice of Intention to Circulate Recall Petition").

     I talked to David Davenport, who is now filing another
Form 410, which will state "O'Blennis Committee, also known
as Legal Defense Fund". From my conversation with Mr. Davenport, I gather the funds were co-mingled. The letter from
the Mayor was sent prior to the Recall Election held November
3, 1981. To further complicate, the O'Blennis Committee is
now holding a St. Patrick's Day Corn Beef and Cabbage Feed to
use up the left over funds from the Legal Defense Fund. On
the evening of January 25, 1982, a $99.00 per plate fund raising dinner was held. The proceeds from that activity have not
been declared yet. The Mayor's case went to court January 25,
1982 and ended March 2, 1982.

     Any help you may give me on this I would appreciate. Mr.
Davenport wants to file whatever is necessary; however, I feel
I need assistance on straightening this situation out, since
all the filing seems to have been late. In addition, I would
like to know what filings are needed from the Mayor, and what
the Mayor should declare, if anything, on his 721 filing for
1981.

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Betty M. Pellinen
                                        City Clerk

City Hall
250 East L Street
Benicia, California 94510
(707) 745-0510

WARREN R. O'BLENNIS
Mayor

Residence
955 West L Street
Benicia, California 94510

October 27, 1981

Dear Friend,

Recently the Benicia Herald has urged me to clarify the issue of my residency and my ability to continue to serve as Mayor because you and I are being asked to make a decision that will effect our community for decades.

You probably know from reading the newspapers that I announced a year ago that my company was transferring my job to another city and that I may have to resign as Mayor. In light of this I purchased a home in Loomis and moved my wife and children there. I didn't want my kids to have to start school in the middle of the year.

While I publicly and privately told people that I planned to stay in Benicia--and I continued to maintain my own residence here at my sister's house--at the time it appeared that eventually I would have to move.

But because I am committed to Benicia and feel an obligation to complete my term of service as Mayor, I have continued to live here and commute to work. That hasn't been easy--but our City is worth it.

As you also probably know, the issue of my ability to serve as Mayor is now before the Superior Court which I believe is the correct forum for resolving this question.

Conditions within my company have changed which now allow me to remain permanently in Benicia and return my family here. Nothing could make me or my family happier. The fault for all this controversy is partially mine. I should not have announced my intention to move so precipitously. But regardless of what you may read in the barrage of campaign literature Winston has produced, I love our city and a dedicated to it. I recently moved into my new home at 955 West L Street.

You must understand how painful this whole controversy of residency has been to my family. My 11 year old daughter's school records were subpoened in court as was my supervisor at work and my personnel file. My family has had to endure immense stress.

Does this make sense? I don't think so.

[illegible]

What makes even less sense is Gary Winston's attempt to use my
residency as a reason why he shouldn't be recalled. The fact is,
the recall election has nothing to do with where I live.

The only question in that election is whether Gary Winston has
performed as well as you have the right to expect. All other issues
are irrelevant.

Personally, I will continue to reside in Benicia and serve as
Mayor to work on our city's behalf. I have great hopes for Benicia
because I know we are a community that strives to achieve.

I appreciate all the support so many people have given me. But
right now, I hope you will make your decision on the recall election
based on a careful consideration of the facts--and not on other,
irrelevant issues.

Sincerely,

Warren O'Blennis
Warren O'Blennis

WARREN R. O'BLENNIS
Mayor
250 East L Street
Benicia, California 94510

BULK RATE
U.S. POSTAGE
PAID

Benicia, CA 94510

URGENT
MESSAGE

900 SOUTHAMPTON ROAD
BENICIA                CA 94510

NOTICE OF INTENTION TO CIRCULATE
RECALL PETITION

Pursuant to Section 27007, California Elections Code, the undersigned
registered qualified voters of the City of Benicia, State of California, hereby
give notice that they are the proponents of a recall petition for the removal
from office of Gary Winston, M.D., City Council member, incumbent, and intend
to circulate said petition in said city for the purpose of procuring signatures
of registered qualified voters thereto.

The grounds for the proposed recall are:

Gary Winston has shown utter disrespect for the democratic principles of
fairness and due process by his repugnant, politically motivated attempt to
oust a fellow duly-elected official.

Winston has paralyzed Benicia's government by exaggerating his own importance and placing himself above the electorate. Like an autocrat above the
constitutional concepts of law and justice, Winston has presided over a
"kangaroo court." He has even improperly tampered with City Council videotapes
of official proceedings.

On April 21, 1981, Winston moved on a surprise, non-agenda item to send
the Mayor's residency issue to the state Attorney General. The motion was brought
forth in disregard for the public's right to know in advance the business of
government, yet it would have sent the issue to an impartial, third party for
review.

On April 22, Winston secretly re-recorded his own speech portion on cable
TV videotapes.

Then, on April 28, Winston moved to vacate the Mayor's seat, exceeding his
power and the City Council's legal power.

The Mayor deserves due process. He is to be believed at his word until a
higher authority, such as a court of law, decides otherwise.

Councilman Winston's blatant disrespect for democratic principles warrants
his removal from office as our representative.

Dated: Benicia, California, May 6th, 1981.

Name                                    Address
Ernest Poole                           1280 West 4 Street Benicia, CA
Ernest Krueger Jr.                     552 Hastings St, Benicia
James Torres                           133 Coal St, Benicia
Dina McArthur                          215 Carlisle Way, Benicia
Paul Ohlman                            399 WEST I ST, Benicia


"Within seven days after the filing of the notice of intention, the
officer sought to be recalled may file with the clerk, or in the case of
a state officer, the Secretary of State, an answer, in not more than 200
words, to the statement of the proponents. If an answer is filed, the officer shall also serve a copy of it, personally or by certified mail, on
one of the proponents named in the notice of intention." (Election Code
Sect. 27023)

This document is a Schedule E form titled "PAYMENTS AND CONTRIBUTIONS MADE" for the period from 7-1-81 through 12-31-81. The form is used to record payments and contributions made by a candidate or committee, in this case, Warren O'Brien's Legal Defense Fund.

The form includes columns for:
- Name and address of payee, creditor, or recipient of contribution
- If contribution check here (with checkboxes)
- Description of expenditure
- Amount paid

The payments listed are as follows:

1. Hugh Comisky, 1930 Sonoma Blvd., Vallejo, CA - Attorney's Fees: $6225
2. Postmaster, Benicia, CA - Postage: $440
3. Overnight Printing, 2256 Palou Ave., S.F., CA - Printing: $620.63

The subtotal of these payments is $7285.63.

The summary section at the bottom of the form shows:

1. Payments of $100 or more made this period (include all Schedule E subtotals): $7285.63
2. Payments under $100 this period (not itemized): $153.77
3. Total accrued expenses paid this period (Schedule F, Line 4): $0
4. Total payments this period (Lines 1 + 2 + 3): $7439.40

Dear Friend,

Recently the Benicia Herald has urged me to clarify the issue of my residency and my ability to continue to serve as Mayor because you and I are being asked to make a decision that will effect our community for decades.

You probably know from reading the newspapers that I announced a year ago that my company was transferring my job to another city and that I may have to resign as Mayor. In light of this I purchased a home in Loomis and moved my wife and children there. I didn't want my kids to have to start school in the middle of the year.

While I publicly and privately told people that I planned to stay in Benicia—and I continued to maintain my own residence here at my sister's house—at the time it appeared that eventually I would have to move.

But because I am committed to Benicia and feel an obligation to complete my term of service as Mayor, I have continued to live here and commute to work. That hasn't been easy—but our City is worth it.

As you also probably know, the issue of my ability to serve as Mayor is now before the Superior Court which I believe is the correct forum for resolving this question.

Conditions within my company have changed which now allow me to remain permanently in Benicia and return my family here. Nothing could make me or my family happier. The fault for all this controversy is partially mine. I should not have announced my intention to move so precipitously. But regardless of what you may read in the barrage of campaign literature Winston has produced, I love our city and am dedicated to it. I recently moved into my new home at 955 West L Street.

You must understand how painful this whole controversy of residency has been to my family. My 11 year old daughter's school records were subpoenaed in court as was my supervisor at work and my personnel file. My family has had to endure immense stress.

Does this make sense? I don't think so.

What makes even less sense is Gary Winston's attempt to use my residency as a reason why he shouldn't be recalled. The fact is, the recall election has nothing to do with where I live.

The only question in that election is whether Gary Winston has performed as well as you have the right to expect. All other issues are irrelevant.

Personally, I will continue to reside in Benicia and serve as Mayor to work on our city's behalf. I have great hopes for Benicia because I know we are a community that strives to achieve.

I appreciate all the support so many people have given me. But right now, I hope you will make your decision on the recall election based on a careful consideration of the facts--and not on other, irrelevant issues.

embedding.first_500_words: March 29, 1982 Betty M. Pellinen City Clerk City Hall 250 East L Street Benicia, CA 94510 Re: Advice Request No. 82-050 Dear Ms. Pellinen: Thank you for your letter requesting advice and assistance from this office. We do not have enough information to judge the sufficiency of the filings of the O'Blennis Committee or the Legal Defense Fund. However, I can provide you with a general outline of the applicable reporting requirements of the Political Reform Act which should assist you in your duties as filing officer. If Mayor O'Blennis would like more specific advice on the filings required from his committees, he or his treasurer should contact us directly or through their legal representative. The Act requires that controlled committees file semi-annual campaign statements reporting all contributions and expenditures during the reporting period. Government Code Section 84200. In addition, threshold campaign statements are required if the committee's activities exceed certain limits outlined in Section 84200(b). On all of these statements, contributions and expenditures of $100 or more must be itemized. Amounts collected and spent for legal fees and costs are generally reportable if the lawsuit relates to the election process or the official's status as an officeholder. See Opinion requested by Douglas Buchanan, 5 FPPC Opinions 14 (No. 78-013, May 1, 1979). It appears that the funds collected 1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. and expended by the Mayor's committee for the defense of the lawsuit brought to remove him from office would be reportable as contributions and expenditures. On the Mayor's annual Statement of Economic Interest, he must report all interests in real property, investments, and income. Section 87203. Income does not include political contributions or expenditures for legal services (which are reportable on the committee's campaign statements). Section 82030(b)(1). Income does include gifts from any source including gifts from a political committee. Sections 82028 and 82030(a). I hope this discussion has been helpful to you. If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. Very truly yours, [signature] Diane Maura Fishburn Counsel Legal Division DMF:plh cc: David Davenport 2/ In this regard, please see Election Code Section 12402 which prohibits the personal use of campaign funds. Any questions concerning the interpretation of this section should be directed to the Attorney General's Office or the Solano County District Attorney. March 12, 1982 Ms. Jean Pritchard Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, Ca 95814 Dear Jean: As per our telephone conversation, I am sending you the Statement of Organization and Recipient Committee Campaign Statement filed by David Davenport on behalf of "Warren O'Blennis Legal Defense Fund"; also, the letter mailed by Warren R. O'Blennis, Mayor (Paid for by O'Blennis Committee). The letter made reference to the Recall having nothing to do with his residence (I am also sending you a copy of the "Notice of Intention to Circulate Recall Petition"). I talked to David Davenport, who is now filing another Form 410, which
